structural_observations: The template fixes schema version 2.0.0, a canonical authority locator and version, nullable criteria and lineage hashes, one unit object, one ten-field psychology object, one evidence object, exactly three repeated review-seat objects labeled A, B, and C, and one approval object.

Unit fields explicitly capture combat role, frontliner state, sex-design category, visual lane, deliberate-horror state, primary and secondary trait channels, gameplay identity, preserved hook, dominant prop or anatomy, protection logic, supernatural cost, and emotion family.

Evidence fields separate a three-option silhouette sheet, selected master, same-master face derivative, deterministic 96-pixel derivative, Vellum-first comparison, three nearest neighbors, and roster boards; the face and 96 records each carry source-master hashes and fixed derivation labels.

Each seat contains one reviewer_id, an independent-record path and hash, fifteen verdict slots, six visible-face fields, a professional-standard block, blockers, readiness, and a cross-examination record path, hash, verdict, surviving blockers, outcome, and protected dissent.
Approval fields store derived gate, readiness, and professional booleans, a blocker ledger, Board status, a user-approved flag, and user-approval provenance.
semantic_observations: The template itself does not define evaluator family, role, session, clean-context, instance, transcript, or sealed-payload identity fields.
It contains no per-artifact inspection receipt, observed-region list, inspection-mode list, criterion-by-criterion evidence binding, or surface-class reconstruction.

Independent round-one and cross-examination content is represented only by external JSON path and hash fields; peer targets, quoted claims, falsification attempts, claim identifiers, and evidence bindings are not modeled inside this template.

Professional-standard fields require one free-text benchmark comparison, rejection case, excellence gap, and an array of dimensions, but the template does not bind benchmark IDs, hashes, surfaces, or evidence IDs.
Most semantic fields are nullable placeholders, so substantive constraints depend on the separate validator rather than being expressed by a JSON schema in this artifact.
uncertainties: External independent-round and cross-examination JSON files may contain provenance and evidence detail absent from the template, but their schemas are not identified here.
The exact object shape expected for user_approval_provenance is not shown in the template because the field is null.
The template does not state whether blocker strings must resolve to a separate claim or finding registry.

structural_observations: The validator enforces schema and authority tokens, enumerated lanes, sex categories, protection options and emotion families, ten required psychology fields, ten universal verdict gates, five conditional gates, six face-evidence fields, five professional bands, and eight professional dimensions.

It derives conditional female, frontline, deliberate-horror, and Blessed applicability from unit metadata and derives each seat's readiness from gate results, lowest professional dimension, evidence confidence, blockers, and cross-examination clearance.

It requires three unique reviewer_id strings, distinct round-one and cross-examination paths and hashes, source-master hash equality for face and 96 derivatives, exactly three unique neighbor IDs, nonempty roster-board evidence, and derived approval fields.
File mode checks artifact existence and SHA-256, inspects PNG signature and IHDR width and height, enforces the 96-by-96 dimensions, and parses review artifacts as nonempty UTF-8 JSON.

The self-test includes negative mutations for failed gates paired with readiness, empty psychology, false conditional inapplicability, derivative-hash mismatch, duplicate reviewer IDs, false aggregates, invalid lanes, horror-lane mismatch, unresolved cross-examination, wrong criteria hash, shared review evidence, placeholder prose, low-confidence readiness, unstructured user approval, and non-image visual evidence.
semantic_observations: At this artifact's actual path, ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] resolves to the repository's docs/art directory; the resulting criteria and default-template locators resolve under docs/art/docs/art and do not exist.

Completed-record validation reads the computed criteria path without handling a missing-file exception inside validate_record, so the current-path locator prevents the criteria-byte comparison from completing.

Reviewer independence is reduced to unique reviewer_id values plus distinct path and byte-hash strings; evaluator family, instance, session, context, transcript, role, and sealed primary payload are not checked.

Round-one and cross-examination JSON files are accepted when they parse to any nonempty object or list; their internal seat identity, peer bindings, challenge completeness, quoted targets, evidence receipts, and semantic dispositions are not validated.

Face, psychology, benchmark, dimension, rejection-case, and excellence-gap evidence is checked primarily by enum membership, sentinel rejection, and minimum character length; no cited evidence or benchmark identifier must resolve.
The required free-text rejection_case and excellence_gap fields must each contain at least forty characters for every seat, including records that otherwise contain no blocker.

The declared crop_and_scale_only and deterministic_containment_only methods are checked as literal strings and source-hash fields, but the derived image pixels are not compared with the selected master.
PNG validation checks the signature and IHDR header but does not decode the complete image stream or require a valid terminal chunk.

The professional band is mechanically tied to the lowest supplied dimension and confidence, but the truth of the supplied dimension evidence and benchmark comparison remains outside the code's checks.
uncertainties: The file may have been copied from a location where parents[2] would resolve to repository root, but the frozen artifact is stored and receipted at the path inspected here.
A separate external schema may validate the referenced round-one or cross-examination JSON records, but this artifact neither invokes nor identifies one.
No completed record was supplied in this evidence scope, so interactions among real blocker strings, external review files, and user-approval provenance were not directly exercised.
